text:
Admission Date:  2004-04-06              Discharge Date:   1974-02-25

Date of Birth:  1972-08-20             Sex:   F

Service: SURGERY

Allergies:
Iodine; Iodine Containing / Codeine / Lanolin

Attending:Jeffrey
Chief Complaint:
Ms. Anderson is a 49-year-old woman who had in the past
undergone an open Roux-en-Y gastric bypass for morbid obesity.
Her current weight is 400 pounds. She could not tell me her pre-

bypass weight. She subsequently developed an incisional hernia,
which evidently was not repaired secondary to her body habitus.
She presented with a 2-day history of abdominal pain with
progressive nausea and ultimately bilious emesis. She did have a

slight leukocytosis and a CAT scan demonstrating a transition
point within her incisional hernia with proximal dilation of
small intestine.

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
hernia repair


History of Present Illness:
49 y/o with morbid obesity w h/o of gastric bypass in 1980 with
ventral hernia. Patient complains of increased pain over hernia
and multiple episodes of nasuea and vomiting. Pain began
yesterday. She denies any fevers or chills. She denies diarrhea.
Her last flatus was yesterday. Intially she was seen at Summerville Medical Center
Hospital then was transferred to Little River Healthcare.


Past Medical History:
 hypothyroid, GERD, s/p hysterectomy, s/p appy, s/p ccy, s/p T&A

Social History:
non contributory

Physical Exam:
99.8 100 120/50  12
NAD
RRR
CTAB
ABD: soft, very large ventral hernia with some tenderness over
hernia, no skin changes, no rebound, no guarding. positive bowel
sounds
Ext: no edema

Pertinent Results:
1971-05-11 CT of abdomen: High grade-distal small bowel obstruction
with the transition point in the large abdominal wall hernia,
which contains patient's colon and most of her  small bowel.
Intact gastric bypass.

01-19 Nasal swab-STAPH AUREUS COAG +,  OXACILLIN sensitive
05-15 urine cx neg, 04-01 bld cx neg, 09-03 urine cx/bld cx no growth




Brief Hospital Course:
On 2004-04-06 patient underwent an ex-laparscopy, lysis of adhesions
and hernia repair.
2 drains were placed. See operative note for details.
Post-operative she was intially in the SICU b/c she remained
intubated. In the SICU she recieved Kefzol for prophylatic
infections coverage, was kept NPO, recieved IV fluids and had an
abdominal binder. She spike a [**Date Range **] on post-operative day 3 and
physical exam revealed a superficial cellulitis right midline.
She was continued on the Kefzol for cellulitis. On
Post-operative day 4 she was transferred to the floor. She then
advanced her diet and worked with physical therapy. She then was
waiting for rehab placement in skilled nursing facility. The
patient's JP drains were discontinued today on POD 11. She will
be continued on keflex for an additional ten days. The reason if
because she had some erythem and cellulitis around her incision
that has largely cleared. The patient is stable at discharge.
She can be continued on a stage V diet. Her Right internal
jugular central line was also removed just prior to discharge.

Medications on Admission:
synthroid, estraderm, prilosec, MVI, vitE, vitC, folic acid,
B12, B6

Discharge Medications:
1. Levothyroxine 50 mcg Tablet Sig: One (1) Tablet PO DAILY
(Daily).
Disp:*30 Tablet(s)* Refills:*2*
2. Oxycodone-Acetaminophen 5-325 mg/5 mL Solution Sig: 5-10 MLs
PO Q4-6H (every 4 to 6 hours) as needed for pain.
Disp:*100 ML(s)* Refills:*0*
3. Cephalexin 500 mg Capsule Sig: One (1) Capsule PO Q6H (every
6 hours) for 10 days.
Disp:*40 Capsule(s)* Refills:*0*


Discharge Disposition:
Extended Care

Facility:
Theda Clark Med Ctr TCU

Discharge Diagnosis:
Ventral Hernia


Discharge Condition:
good


Discharge Instructions:
Please call doctor Jaclyn Ramirez greater than 101, nasuea/vomiting,
inability to eat, wound redness/warmth/swelling/foul smelling
discharge, abdominal pain not controlled by pain medications or
any otehr concerns.Resume taking all medications as taken prior
to this surgery and pain medications and stool softner as
prescribed. Please follow up as directed. No heavy lifting for 6
wke. 04 leave wound open to air please leave steri-strips
intact until they fall off.

Followup Instructions:
Please call Dr. Hoover for a follow up
appointment.(995-381-120
